author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
Scriptural Instances Wherein It is Proved that God Has Men’s Wills More in His Power Than They Themselves Have.
title: Scriptural Instances Wherein It is Proved that God Has Men’s Wills More in His Power Than They Themselves Have.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: It is not, then, to be doubted that men’s wills cannot, so as to prevent His doing what he wills, withstand the will of God, “who hath done all things whatsoever He pleased in heaven and in earth,” and who also “has done those things that are to come;” since He does even concerning the wills themselves of men what He will, when He will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Unless, perchance (to mention some things among many), when God willed to give the kingdom to Saul, it was so in the power of the Israelites, as it certainly was placed in their will, either to subject themselves or not to the man in question, that they could even prevail to withstand God.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: God, however, did not do this, save by the will of the men themselves, because he beyond doubt had the most omnipotent power of inclining men’s hearts whither it pleased Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For thus it is written: “And Samuel sent the people away, and every one went away unto his own place.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And Saul went away to his house in Gibeah: and there went away with Saul mighty men, whose hearts the Lord touched.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And pestilent children said, Who shall save us?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And they despised him, and brought him no presents.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Will any one say that any of those whose hearts the Lord touched to go with Saul would not have gone with him, or that any of those pestilent fellows, whose hearts He did not touch to do this, would have gone?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Of David also, whom the Lord ordained to the kingdom in a more prosperous succession, we read thus: “And David continued to increase, and was magnified, and the Lord was with him.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: This having been premised, it is said a little afterwards, “And the Spirit clothed Amasai, chief of the thirty, and he said, We are thine, O David, and we will be with thee, O son of Jesse: Peace, peace be unto thee, and peace be to thy helpers; because the Lord has helped thee.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Could he withstand the will of God, and not rather do the will of Him who wrought in his heart by His Spirit, with which he was clothed, to will, speak, and do thus?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Moreover, a little afterwards the same Scripture says, “All these warlike men, setting the battle in array, came with a peaceful heart to Hebron to establish David over all Israel.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: By their own will, certainly, they appointed David king.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Who cannot see this?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Who can deny it?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For they did not do it under constraint or without good-will, since they did it with a peaceful heart.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And yet He wrought this in them who worketh what He will in the hearts of men.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: For which reason the Scripture premised, “And David continued to increase, and was magnified, and the Lord Omnipotent was with him.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And thus the Lord Omnipotent, who was with him, induced these men to appoint him king.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And how did He induce them?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Did He constrain thereto by any bodily fetters?

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: He wrought within; He held their hearts; He stirred their hearts, and drew them by their own wills, which He Himself wrought in them.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: If, then, when God wills to set up kings in the earth, He has the wills of men more in His power than they themselves have, who else causes rebuke to be wholesome and correction to result in the heart of him that is rebuked, that he may be established in the kingdom of heaven?
